title: molinia caerulea subsp. litoralis
intro: Molinia caerulea subsp. litoralis, also known as sea sweet-grass or sea molinia, is a subspecies of the Molinia caerulea plant, a type of perennial grass that is native to the coastal regions of Europe and Asia. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant properties. The health benefits of Molinia caerulea subsp. litoralis include its ability to help alleviate respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its potential to aid in wound healing and reduce inflammation. Molinia caerulea subsp. litoralis can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are commonly used to harness its medicinal properties.
